title: Manual Setting of K-mer Coverage Peaks for Meta-Velvetg
author: Afiahayati
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d7k9kv

steps.title: Execute Velvet Tools
steps.instruction: Execute velveth, velvetg, and meta-velvetg as described in the "Getting started" protocol. Please check that "out-dir/meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt" is successfully created.

steps.title: Generate K-mer Coverage Histogram in R
steps.instruction: Draw a k-mer coverage histogram and manually determine the k-mer coverage peaks using R. First, start R. Install and load the 'plotrix' package if you haven't already by running: install.packages("plotrix") and library(plotrix). Then, load the data from the generated file: data = read.table("out-dir/meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt", header=TRUE). Finally, generate the weighted histogram: weighted.hist(data$short1_cov, data$lgth, breaks=seq(0, 200, by=1)).

steps.title: Determine Coverage Peak Values
steps.instruction: Manually determine the k-mer coverage peak values from the generated histogram. For example, seven coverage peaks might be observed (around 210x, 120x, 70x, 45x, 23x, 12x, and 6x). If errors occur during this determination, consult the 'Trouble shootings' section for assistance.

steps.title: Run Meta-Velvetg with Custom Peaks
steps.instruction: Run meta-velvetg with the manually determined coverage peak values. An example command would be: meta-velvetg out-dir -ins_length 260 -exp_covs 214_122_70_43_25_13.5. Please note that meta-velvetg assumes that the peak values provided must be sorted in descending order.
